scope.request: audio brief script from fixture Feed Items

time_range.label: Fixture Feed Items for audio brief script eval

scope.selection_rule: Grouped the fixture Feed Items into exactly two Artifact Topic units: one for show-style story setup and two-host structure, and one for post-review server-render readiness.

units.title: A useful audio brief starts with story setup and a real second host
units.claim: The script should open like a show, explain what happened and why it matters, then use the second host to ask clarifying questions instead of repeating the lead.

supporting_evidence.title: Audio scripts need story setup before analysis
supporting_evidence.reason: The item states that strong sections open with concrete events, listener relevance, and host turns that clarify the story.

supporting_evidence.title: Second hosts should ask useful clarifying questions
supporting_evidence.reason: The item says the second host should act as a listener proxy and translate dense claims into spoken meaning.

units.selection_rationale: This is the lead unit because it defines the editorial behavior the Show Script must demonstrate before any server-rendered audio submission is considered.

units.title: Reviewed scripts become server-rendered audio definitions
units.claim: After Script Review is ready, the Skill submits the reviewed Show Script bundle to api instead of creating local segment manifests or local audio files.

supporting_evidence.title: Server rendering owns audio segmentation
supporting_evidence.reason: The item explains that api derives render segments after submission and owns retry, assembly, metadata, storage, and delivery.

units.selection_rationale: This unit is included because the requested path stops at the reviewed script and bundle-input boundary before server rendering.
